childhood.familyBackground: Son of Klaus Friedrich Thiel (chemical engineer) and Susanne Thiel. Younger brother Patrick Michael Thiel. Five generations of ancestors traced to Scottish immigrants to Australia.
childhood.familyBackgroundKo: 화학공학자인 아버지 클라우스 프리드리히 틸과 어머니 수잔 틸의 아들. 동생은 패트릭 마이클 틸. 5대조가 호주로 이민한 스코틀랜드 이민자로부터 후손
childhood.education: San Mateo High School (valedictorian, 1985). Stanford University (B.A. Philosophy, 1989). Stanford Law School (J.D., 1992). Founded The Stanford Review newspaper critical of political correctness.
childhood.educationKo: 샌 마테오 고등학교(1985년 수석졸업). 스탠포드 대학 철학과 학사(1989년). 스탠포드 법학대학원 법학박사(1992년). 정치적 올바름을 비판하는 스탠포드 리뷰 신문 창립
childhood.earlyLife: Immigrated to US at age 1, lived in Cleveland, Ohio initially. Frequent moves to South Africa, Namibia before settling Foster City, California in 1977. Changed elementary schools seven times. Chess prodigy, peak USCF rating 2342 in 1992. Won statewide math competition in California middle school.
childhood.earlyLifeKo: 1세에 미국으로 이민하여 초기에 오하이오 클리블랜드에 거주. 남아프리카, 나미비아로 자주 이사한 후 1977년 캘리포니아 포스터 시티에 정착. 초등학교를 7번 전학. 체스 천재로 1992년 USCF 최고 등급 2342를 기록. 캘리포니아 중학교 수학 경시대회 우승

careerTimeline.event: Graduated Stanford University with B.A. in Philosophy
Graduated Stanford Law School; published The Diversity Myth co-authored with David Sacks
careerTimeline.eventKo: 스탠포드 법학대학원 졸업. 데이비드 색스와 공저한 '다양성의 신화' 출간

careerTimeline.event: Worked as clerk, securities lawyer, speechwriter, and derivatives trader at Credit Suisse
careerTimeline.eventKo: 크레디트 스위스에서 법원 조수, 증권 변호사, 연설문 작성자, 파생상품 거래자로 일함

careerTimeline.event: Co-founded PayPal with Max Levchin and Luke Nosek
careerTimeline.eventKo: 막스 레브친, 루크 노섹과 함께 페이팔 공동 창립

careerTimeline.event: PayPal sold to eBay for $1.5 billion; Thiel netted approximately $55 million
careerTimeline.eventKo: 페이팔을 이베이에 15억 달러에 매각. 틸이 약 5,500만 달러 수익

careerTimeline.event: Founded Clarium Capital, a global macro hedge fund based in San Francisco
careerTimeline.eventKo: 샌프란시스코 기반 글로벌 거시경제 헤지펀드 클라리움 캐피탈 설립

careerTimeline.event: Incorporated Palantir Technologies, a big data analysis company
careerTimeline.eventKo: 빅데이터 분석 회사 팔란티르 테크놀로지스 설립

careerTimeline.event: Invested $500,000 in Facebook for 10.2% stake; joined Facebook's board of directors
careerTimeline.eventKo: 페이스북에 50만 달러를 투자하여 10.2% 지분 획득. 페이스북 이사회 입석

careerTimeline.event: Launched Founders Fund with PayPal partners Ken Howery, Luke Nosek focusing on technology and hard tech investments
careerTimeline.eventKo: 페이팔 동료 켄 하우리, 루크 노섹과 함께 기술 및 첨단 기술 투자에 초점을 맞춘 파운더스 펀드 출범

careerTimeline.event: Sold majority Facebook stake; founded Mithril Capital Management ($402M at launch) targeting growth-stage companies
careerTimeline.eventKo: 페이스북 주식 대량 매각. 초기 자금 4억 200만 달러로 성장 단계 기업을 타겟하는 미스릴 캐피탈 매니지먼트 설립

careerTimeline.event: Left Facebook's board of directors after 18 years

failures.description: Clarium Capital down 4.5% as financial markets collapsed; assets peaked at $8 billion in Q1 2008 amid global financial crisis
failures.descriptionKo: 금융시장이 붕괴하면서 클라리움 캐피탈이 4.5% 하락. 글로벌 금융위기 속 2008년 1분기 자산이 80억 달러 정점
failures.lesson: Even brilliant macro strategists cannot predict all market downturns; concentration in single strategy creates vulnerability
failures.lessonKo: 뛰어난 거시경제 전략가도 모든 시장 하락을 예측할 수는 없음. 단일 전략 집중은 취약성을 만듦

failures.description: Clarium Capital down 25% following 2008 losses; client redemptions accelerated as investor confidence deteriorated
failures.descriptionKo: 2008년 손실에 이어 클라리움 캐피탈이 25% 하락. 투자자 신뢰가 악화되면서 고객 환급 요청 급증
failures.lesson: Losses compound when investors lose faith; redemptions reduce capital for recovery strategies and force liquidations
failures.lessonKo: 투자자들이 신뢰를 잃으면 손실이 복합적으로 증가. 환급으로 회복 전략을 위한 자본이 줄어들고 청산을 초래

failures.description: Clarium Capital down 23% continuing downward trend; fund assets declined from $8 billion to $350 million by 2011
failures.descriptionKo: 클라리움 캐피탈이 23% 하락하며 하락세 계속됨. 펀드 자산이 80억 달러에서 2011년까지 3억 5천만 달러로 감소
failures.lesson: Consecutive losses in hedge funds trigger accelerating withdrawals; better to admit strategy failure and transition than attempt recovery
failures.lessonKo: 헤지펀드의 연속 손실은 가속화하는 이탈을 초래. 회복 시도보다 전략 실패를 인정하고 전환하는 것이 나음

quotes.text: The next Bill Gates will not build an operating system. The next Larry Page or Sergey Brin won't make a search engine. And the next Mark Zuckerberg won't create a social network. If you are copying these guys, you aren't learning from them.
quotes.textKo: 다음 빌 게이츠는 운영체제를 만들지 않을 것이다. 다음 래리 페이지나 세르게이 브린은 검색엔진을 만들지 않을 것이다. 다음 마크 저커버그는 소셜 네트워크를 만들지 않을 것이다. 이런 사람들을 모방하고 있다면, 그들로부터 배우고 있는 것이 아니다.
quotes.context: Zero to One - on innovation and avoiding imitation
quotes.contextKo: 제로 투 원에서 - 혁신과 모방 회피에 대하여

quotes.text: All happy companies are different: each one earns a monopoly by solving a unique problem. All failed companies are the same: they failed to escape competition.
quotes.textKo: 모든 성공한 기업은 다르다: 각각은 독특한 문제를 해결함으로써 독점을 얻는다. 모든 실패한 기업은 같다: 경쟁에서 벗어나지 못했다.
quotes.context: Zero to One - on competitive advantage and monopoly
quotes.contextKo: 제로 투 원에서 - 경쟁우위와 독점에 대하여

quotes.text: What important truth do very few people agree with you on?
quotes.textKo: 매우 적은 사람들이 당신과 동의하는 중요한 진실이 무엇인가?
quotes.context: Contrarian question used as job interview standard to identify original thinking
quotes.contextKo: 독창적 사고를 식별하기 위해 채용 면접 기준으로 사용되는 역발상적 질문

quotes.text: Every great business is built around a secret that's hidden from the outside. A great company is a conspiracy to change the world.
quotes.textKo: 모든 위대한 기업은 외부에 숨겨진 비밀을 중심으로 구축된다. 위대한 회사는 세상을 바꾸기 위한 음모이다.
quotes.context: Zero to One - on proprietary business secrets and mission-driven companies
quotes.contextKo: 제로 투 원에서 - 독점적 비즈니스 비밀과 미션 중심 회사에 대하여

quotes.text: The single most powerful pattern I have noticed is that successful people find value in unexpected places, and they do this by thinking about business from first principles instead of formulas.
quotes.textKo: 내가 주목한 가장 강력한 패턴은 성공한 사람들이 공식 대신 첫 원리로부터 비즈니스를 생각함으로써 예기치 않은 곳에서 가치를 찾는다는 것이다.
quotes.context: On competitive advantage through original thinking and pattern recognition
quotes.contextKo: 독창적 사고와 패턴 인식을 통한 경쟁우위에 대하여

authored.title: The Diversity Myth: Multiculturalism and Political Intolerance on Campus
authored.note: Co-authored with David O. Sacks, published 1995. Critical of multiculturalism at Stanford University. Both authors later distanced themselves from controversial arguments.
authored.noteKo: 데이비드 색스와 공저, 1995년 출간. 스탠포드 대학의 다문화주의 비판. 두 저자 모두 나중에 논쟁적 주장과 거리를 둠

authored.title: Zero to One: Notes on Startups, or How to Build the Future
authored.note: Co-authored with Blake Masters, published 2014. Seminal work on creating breakthrough innovation through monopoly building and contrarian thinking. Became essential reading in Silicon Valley.
authored.noteKo: 블레이크 마스터스와 공저, 2014년 출간. 독점 구축과 역발상적 사고를 통한 혁신 창출에 관한 기념비적 저작. 실리콘밸리의 필독서가 됨

recommended.title: Things Hidden Since the Foundation of the World
recommended.author: René Girard
recommended.why: Girard's mimetic theory profoundly influenced Thiel's thinking about human behavior, competition, and business strategy; provides sweeping view of human experience
recommended.whyKo: 지라르의 모방 이론이 인간 행동, 경쟁, 비즈니스 전략에 대한 틸의 사고에 깊은 영향. 인간 경험의 포괄적 관점 제시

recommended.title: Lord of the Rings
recommended.author: J.R.R. Tolkien
recommended.why: Thiel's favorite novel; influenced his naming of Palantir (Tolkien artifact) and Mithril Capital (Tolkien metal); reflects his love of fantasy and world-building
recommended.whyKo: 틸의 가장 사랑하는 소설. 팔란티르(톨킨 아티팩트)와 미스릴 캐피탈(톨킨 금속) 명명에 영향. 판타지와 세계 창조에 대한 애정 반영

recommended.title: The New Atlantis
recommended.author: Francis Bacon
recommended.why: Part of Thiel's interest in 'past books written about the future'; represents vision of technological progress and scientific advancement
recommended.whyKo: 틸의 '과거에 미래에 대해 쓴 책들' 관심의 일부. 기술 진보와 과학 발전의 비전을 나타냄

recommended.title: The Diamond Age
recommended.author: Neal Stephenson
recommended.why: Science fiction exploring nanotechnology and social structures; aligns with Thiel's interest in technology-driven future scenarios
recommended.whyKo: 나노기술과 사회 구조를 탐구하는 과학소설. 틸의 기술 주도 미래 시나리오 관심과 부합

recommended.title: The Hard Thing About Hard Things
recommended.author: Ben Horowitz
recommended.why: Thiel values Horowitz's practical insights on building startups and leadership through adversity
recommended.whyKo: 틸이 스타트업 구축과 역경을 통한 리더십에 관한 호로위츠의 실용적 통찰을 높이 평가

personalTraits.knownFor: Contrarian thinking, identifying undervalued opportunities, first-principles analysis, unconventional views on technology and society, chess prodigy intellect, articulate philosophical perspective, backing founders like Mark Zuckerberg early
personalTraits.knownForKo: 역발상적 사고, 저평가된 기회 발굴, 첫 원리 분석, 기술과 사회에 대한 비관례적 관점, 체스 천재의 지능, 명확한 철학적 관점, 마크 저커버그 같은 초기 창업자 지원
personalTraits.philanthropy: Thiel Fellowship program (grants to young entrepreneurs to skip college and pursue startups). Support for AI safety research through various initiatives. Contributions to longevity and life extension research. Medical innovation funding.
personalTraits.philanthropyKo: 틸 펠로우십 프로그램(젊은 기업가들에게 대학을 건너뛰고 스타트업을 추구할 수 있게 하는 보조금). 다양한 이니셔티브를 통한 AI 안전 연구 지원. 장수 및 생명 연장 연구 기여. 의료 혁신 자금 지원
personalTraits.controversies: The Diversity Myth arguments criticized as misrepresenting multiculturalism; later distanced from views. Support for Trump's 2016 presidential campaign. Lawsuits against Gawker media (2016 victory). Libertarian-leaning political positions viewed as controversial by progressives. Appearances alongside contentious political figures.
personalTraits.controversiesKo: '다양성의 신화' 주장이 다문화주의를 잘못 표현했다고 비판받음. 나중에 관점과 거리를 둠. 트럼프의 2016 대선 지지. 고커 미디어 상대 소송(2016년 승리). 자유주의 경향의 정치 입장이 진보 진영에서 논쟁 대상. 논쟁적 정치인들과의 동석

pivotalMentors.name: René Girard
pivotalMentors.relationship: Intellectual influence via Girard's writings on mimetic theory and human behavior
pivotalMentors.influence: Shaped Thiel's understanding of competition, human desire, and business strategy; framework for seeing patterns others miss

pivotalMentors.name: Dr. Peter Attia
pivotalMentors.influence: Guides Thiel's approach to fitness, biohacking, and long-term health optimization

pivotalMentors.name: J.R.R. Tolkien (literary/intellectual)
pivotalMentors.relationship: Literary and philosophical influence through works
pivotalMentors.influence: Inspired Thiel's naming conventions (Palantir, Mithril Capital), vision of world-building and secondary creation

additionalInsights.dailyRoutine: Sleeps 6-7 hours nightly, in bed around midnight. Mornings focused on deep thinking rather than emails or social media, protecting cognitive bandwidth for high-leverage intellectual work. Reads widely across history, philosophy, science, and political theory. Engages in long, in-depth conversations with intellectuals and entrepreneurs. Maintains ketogenic diet (bulletproof coffee with butter and MCT oil). Regular exercise with personal trainers emphasizing compound movements (deadlifts, kettlebells, HIIT). No structured daily schedule but driven by goals and deal opportunities. Often loses track of time in intellectual discussions.
keyAnecdotes: As chess prodigy, achieved 2342 USCF rating by age 25, demonstrating early mastery of strategic thinking and pattern recognition
Founded The Stanford Review newspaper while student to counter what he viewed as political intolerance, showing early contrarian instincts
Invested $500,000 in Facebook when valued at only $4.9M, turning investment into over $1 billion - exemplifying his ability to identify undervalued opportunities
Directly contacted Mark Zuckerberg with investment offer when Facebook was seeking traditional VC funding, demonstrating boldness and unconventional approach
Asked candidates 'What important truth do very few people agree with you on?' as standard interview question, using philosophy to identify original thinkers
Suffered significant losses in Clarium Capital hedge fund (down 25%, 23%, and 90%) but leveraged experience to refocus on venture capital where he excelled
additionalInsights.turningPoint: The 2004 Facebook investment marked Thiel's transition from individual trading (PayPal wealth) and macro trading (Clarium losses) to venture capital strategy. Recognizing his Clarium losses around 2008-2011, he pivoted entirely toward venture capital through Founders Fund and Mithril Capital, where his first-principles thinking and pattern recognition proved far more valuable than macro prediction. This shift aligned with his natural strengths and transformed venture capital into his primary wealth-creation engine.
additionalInsights.managementStyle: Hands-on but non-micromanaging investor who asks penetrating questions to test founder thinking. Values original contrarian perspectives and first-principles analysis over conventional wisdom. Creates environments that reward bold ideas and intellectual honesty. Willing to back unpopular bets if logic is sound. Focuses on identifying and backing great founders rather than controlling operations. Maintains philosophical approach to business rather than purely financial. Encourages team members to think independently and challenge assumptions. Direct communicator who values clarity and can be blunt about disagreement.
